title: fleurya interrupta
intro: Fleurya interrupta, also known as the Indian fleabane or fleabane plant, is a flowering plant species native to the Indian subcontinent. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fleurya interrupta include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake venom. Fleurya interrupta can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health issues.
